matches.match_rationale: Collection staff need personalized outreach messages (SMS, email, letter) tailored to account status, amount owed, payment history, and family context. Generating these manually at scale across 2,000+ accounts is impractical. AI can draft contextually appropriate communications — firm for chronic non-payers, gentle for families with subsidy delays — reducing the emotional friction caused by generic or incorrect reminders.
matches.opportunity_sketch: An AI-drafted outreach module that generates personalized SMS/email templates per account segment (amount owed, days overdue, prior contact history), ready for staff review before send. Could be embedded in the collections dashboard.

matches.match_rationale: The core triage problem — which of 2,000+ overdue accounts to prioritize today — is a structured classification task. Input features include amount owed, days overdue, prior payment behavior, contact history, and funding source (private vs. government-subsidy). AI can classify each account into priority tiers (urgent escalation, active follow-up, low-value hold) replacing the manual judgment calls that currently lead to wasted effort on PHP 2k accounts.
matches.opportunity_sketch: A daily priority queue that classifies each outstanding account into action tiers, surfaced to collection staff as a ranked worklist. Rules-based scoring exists today but AI classification can incorporate behavioral patterns that rule sets miss (e.g., families who promise and don't pay vs. genuinely delayed subsidy cases).

matches.match_rationale: Not all delinquent accounts behave the same way. A propensity model trained on historical collection outcomes can predict which families are likely to pay given a reminder, which need a payment plan offer, and which are likely to default regardless. This directly addresses the problem of staff spending equal effort on PHP 2k and PHP 50k accounts — effort should correlate with both amount AND predicted collectability.
matches.opportunity_sketch: A 'collectability score' per account that combines balance size, payment history, subsidy status, and prior contact outcomes to predict probability of collection within 30/60/90 days. Used to weight the priority queue and guide staff on which accounts warrant phone calls vs. automated reminders.

matches.match_rationale: The collections workflow is a multi-step process: check payment status across channels → update account → determine next action → log outcome → schedule follow-up → escalate if needed. Today this is manual, leading to staff calling families who already paid and no structured escalation path. A task automation agent can orchestrate this workflow end-to-end: pulling payment updates, refreshing balances, triggering appropriate outreach, logging promises, and escalating overdue escalation accounts without human intervention at each step.
matches.opportunity_sketch: An automated collections workflow agent that runs nightly: reconciles payments from all channels, updates account statuses, sends first-tier automated reminders for small balances, surfaces high-value or escalation-ready accounts to human staff with a full contact history, and logs promised payment dates for follow-up scheduling.

matches.match_rationale: A significant portion of collection follow-up is repetitive: confirming balances, explaining what is owed, offering standard payment plan options, and capturing payment commitments. A conversational AI agent (SMS/chat-based) can handle this first-tier interaction for families with straightforward balances, freeing human staff for complex negotiations and escalations. This also reduces emotional friction from incorrect balances — the AI can pull live data rather than reciting a stale spreadsheet figure.
matches.opportunity_sketch: An SMS or web chat bot that families can query to check their current balance, confirm recent payments, and request or confirm a standard payment plan. Escalates to a human agent when the balance is disputed, the account is flagged for legal, or the family requests a non-standard arrangement.

matches.match_rationale: Payments arrive through multiple channels (bank transfer, GCash, over-the-counter, post-dated checks) and are recorded in inconsistent formats across systems. The root cause of staff calling families who already paid is that these payment records aren't reconciled into a single clean account view. AI-assisted data transformation can normalize and merge payment records from disparate sources into a unified account status, eliminating the stale-data problem.
matches.opportunity_sketch: An ETL pipeline with AI-assisted schema mapping that ingests payment records from multiple channels (bank files, mobile payment APIs, manual entry logs) and normalizes them into a single account balance record updated in near-real-time. The prerequisite infrastructure layer that makes every other collection AI solution accurate.
